policy: 0.3 — image-count threshold for cruise-ship First-Look carousel
method: consult.py — GPT (expand) + Perplexity (expand) — see audit-reports/orchestra/2026-04-29-policies-0.1-0.4.json for orchestra-format equivalent on policies 0.1/0.4

perplexity_recommendation.domain: general ecommerce; cruise/travel-specific lower because hotel travel UX research focuses on room counts not ship complexity

citations.finding: Apparel/accessories need 5-15 thumbnails for full visual inspection. 2 additional minimum, 3+ optimal.
Thumbnails essential for discovering additional images; dot/text indicators alone cause 76% of users to miss key product details.
One product view rarely adequate; use multiple/animated views (rotated, details, enlarged, in-use/context images).
Multiple views like alternate angles, closeups, back views, texture details.
Property/room imaging requires different image types, video supplementation, gallery UI considerations (navigation, zoom, carousels).

reconciled_recommendation.rationale: User chose 8 originally; defensible per GPT (in research-supported 8-12 plateau range). Cruise ships justify upper-end of Baymard's 5-15 apparel range because they have more visual surface area (multiple decks, dining venues, staterooms, exterior views). Max 20 (GPT) preferred over my original 24 — research literature treats ~15-20 as cognitive-load ceiling.
reconciled_recommendation.diversity_detection: Match alt-text against keyword sets: {balcony, suite, oceanview, interior, cabin}, {exterior, hull, profile, pier, port}, {dining, restaurant, buffet, food}, {stateroom, room, bedroom}
